summary: Postinstall hook prints probe message; no installer-side harm in this version
details: The package declares a postinstall lifecycle hook (`node./dist/cjs/seed.cjs`) whose only effect is setting an internal flag and printing `[trap:prepare] hook executed`. There is no network I/O, no filesystem writes outside the package, no shell execution, no credential or environment-variable reads. The `[trap:...]` naming and otherwise-pointless lifecycle script strongly suggest a research / proof-of-concept / dependency-confusion canary rather than a useful library feature. Current bytes are benign for installers, but the maintainer is intentionally exercising lifecycle execution on installer machines, which warrants human awareness in case future versions add real payload.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
